About

We don't issue reports.
We deliver outcomes.

Regulatory transformation, programme delivery, and operating model design. Senior consultants only. No junior teams. No off-the-shelf frameworks.

Too many consulting engagements end with a report and a handshake. SEvans Advisory was founded to change that to provide genuinely senior, embedded expertise that owns the outcome, not just the advice.

Our consultants embed directly within your organisation, own the delivery governance, and stay until the outcome is real. We are the action.

SEvans Advisory was founded by Silvia Evans, whose career was built inside global financial institutions including Goldman Sachs, Barclays, Deutsche Bank, and ING. She knows what good looks like and built SEvans Advisory to deliver it.

Capability 01

Regulatory Change &
Compliance Transformation

Deep expertise built across large-scale KYC, AML, and financial crime programmes inside tier-one banks. We understand both the regulatory requirement and the operational reality of making compliance work at scale.

Regulatory change is rarely just a compliance exercise. It touches people, processes, technology, and culture simultaneously. Most organisations underestimate that complexity and most consultancies underdeliver because they advise from the outside.

SEvans Advisory embeds directly within your compliance and operations teams. We bring the programme structure, the regulatory depth, and the delivery ownership that large-scale change requires.

We do not hand over a framework and leave. We stay until the change is embedded, the team is capable, and the outcome is real.

What This Covers
  • KYC programme delivery policy design, system implementation, team build-out
  • AML workflow redesign and financial crime prevention frameworks
  • MiFID gap analysis and regulatory change implementation
  • Compliance operating model design lean, scalable, regulation-ready
  • End-to-end regulatory change programme management
  • SME workshops and distributed stakeholder management
  • Requirements capture, BRDs, and functional specifications
  • Benefits realisation tracking against agreed baselines
Relevant Sectors
Financial ServicesTier-one banks, challenger banks, payment institutions
FX & Capital MarketsTrading platforms, brokers, market infrastructure
FintechRegulated technology firms navigating compliance at scale
Typical Engagements
KYC TransformationEnd-to-end programme from policy redesign to go-live
Regulatory Gap AnalysisAssessment against current and incoming requirements
Compliance Operating ModelDesign and implementation of scalable compliance function
AML Programme DeliveryWorkflow redesign and financial crime framework build

Capability 02

Programme &
Project Delivery

Senior PM and BA capability for programmes that need steady hands. We own governance, manage cross-functional complexity, and keep teams aligned under pressure from discovery through to go-live and stabilisation.

Complex programmes fail for predictable reasons: governance gaps, misaligned stakeholders, requirements that drift, and delivery teams without clear ownership. SEvans Advisory removes those failure points by embedding senior PM and BA resource directly into the programme.

We take ownership of the governance structure, the backlog, the risk register, and the stakeholder relationships. We keep the programme moving when it wants to stall.

Our clients do not need to manage us. We manage the programme. They see the outcome.

What This Covers
  • Embedded programme leadership from discovery through go-live and stabilisation
  • Agile and hybrid delivery management, backlog ownership, sprint governance
  • Risk, issue, and dependency management with clear escalation paths
  • SDLC alignment requirements traced through design, build, and test
  • Cross-functional stakeholder management and senior reporting
  • Business analysis and requirements engineering throughout delivery
  • AI-augmented requirements capture and documentation
  • Benefits realisation monitoring against agreed baselines
Relevant Sectors
Financial ServicesRegulatory and technology transformation programmes
Capital MarketsPlatform build, migration, and compliance programmes
Commercial SecuritySensitive, high-stakes programme environments
Regulated EnvironmentsAny complex, multi-stakeholder programme

Capability 03

Operating
Model Design

Target operating models that are fit for purpose, commercially grounded, and capable of surviving contact with reality. Delivered with senior stakeholder sign-off not just a slide deck.

Most operating model work fails at the same point: the gap between design and implementation. A target operating model is only valuable if it can actually be delivered if it accounts for the organisation's real constraints, its people, its regulatory environment, and its commercial reality.

SEvans Advisory designs operating models from the inside. We conduct a rigorous current state assessment, engage the right stakeholders, and build a target model that has genuine senior buy-in before implementation begins.

We establish the benefits baseline at inception and track delivery against it. Our clients know what they committed to and can see whether they are achieving it.

What This Covers
  • Current state assessment across global functions and jurisdictions
  • Target operating model design with functional sequencing and service levels
  • Intake standards design and capacity modelling
  • Senior stakeholder engagement and sign-off management
  • Benefits baseline established at inception and tracked through delivery
  • Transition planning and implementation governance
  • Process mapping across people, process, and technology dimensions
  • Post-implementation review and benefits verification
Relevant Sectors
Financial ServicesCompliance, operations, and technology functions
Capital MarketsTrading operations and post-trade processing
Commercial SecurityOperational redesign in complex environments
Regulated EnvironmentsAny function requiring structured operational change
Typical Engagements
Compliance TOMEnd-to-end design of compliance operating model
Operations RedesignCurrent state to target state across a business function
Post-Merger IntegrationOperating model alignment following M&A activity
Function BuildDesign and stand-up of a new organisational function

Capability 04

AI & Automation
Readiness

Most organisations know they should be using AI. Few know where to start, what's realistic, and what will actually stick. We give you a clear, independent answer before any technology decisions are made.

The conversation around AI in financial services has moved faster than most organisations' ability to act on it. There is pressure from leadership, noise from vendors, and a genuine risk of investing in the wrong places or missing the right ones entirely.

SEvans Advisory approaches AI and automation readiness the same way we approach everything: from the inside. We embed within your organisation, map your actual processes, and identify where automation and AI can deliver measurable, sustainable value and where they cannot.

We are independent of any technology vendor. Our recommendations are based on your operational reality, not on what any platform can sell you. The output is a prioritised, commercially grounded roadmap that your organisation can act on with confidence.

We identify the opportunity. We define what good looks like. We stay to oversee implementation if required.

What This Covers
  • Current state process assessment mapping manual, repetitive, and high-friction workflows
  • AI and automation opportunity identification across people, process, and technology
  • Prioritised roadmap ranked by value, feasibility, and implementation risk
  • Vendor-independent technology evaluation and selection support
  • AI readiness assessment data quality, governance, and organisational capability
  • Business case development with quantified benefits and investment requirements
  • Implementation governance and delivery oversight
  • Change management and team capability build
Relevant Sectors
Financial ServicesCompliance, operations, and client-facing process automation
FX & Capital MarketsTrade processing, reporting, and reconciliation automation
Regulated EnvironmentsAny process-heavy regulated function with automation potential
FintechAI integration within existing technology platforms
Typical Engagements
AI Opportunity AssessmentEnd-to-end process review identifying automation candidates
Automation RoadmapPrioritised programme with business cases for each initiative
Technology SelectionIndependent evaluation of AI and automation platforms

Our Work

Case Studies &
Use Cases

Real engagements. Real outcomes. The work below covers both SEvans Advisory client engagements and prior consultant experience brought by our founder clearly distinguished throughout.

SEvans Advisory Engagements

Delivered by SEvans Advisory

Case Study — SEvans Advisory Engagement 01

Client Onboarding and CRM Transformation

Fintech / Foreign ExchangeBusiness AnalysisCRM Transformation
The Situation

Customer drop-off was occurring at two points: during sign-up, and again post-onboarding before the first deposit. The CRM was not capturing these patterns, leaving revenue leakage undiagnosed. Technology and operations were not aligned, and the business lacked the visibility or capability to act.

The Task

Conduct an end-to-end analysis of the client lifecycle and technology architecture. Identify where and why clients were dropping off. Redesign the journey and CRM infrastructure to close the gaps. Embed the solution to ensure it was sustained post delivery.

Our Approach
  • Customer journey mapping from sign-up through to first deposit, identifying friction points at each stage
  • As-is process analysis across customer experience and CRM technology architecture
  • Gap analysis across people, process, and technology dimensions
  • MoSCoW prioritisation framework applied to structure and sequence requirements
  • CRM redesign mapping customers through defined lifecycle stages with automated triggers and drop-off alerts
  • SDLC-aligned delivery with full requirements traceability through design, build, and test
  • BRDs and functional specifications covering stakeholder needs, process gaps, and recommended solutions
  • Cross-functional stakeholder management across technology, operations, and senior leadership
  • Benefits realisation tracking and outcome monitoring against agreed baselines
  • Internal capability building to sustain the new processes independently
The Outcome
✓22% increase in client deposits post-implementation
✓End-to-end visibility of the client onboarding funnel established for the first time
✓Redesigned journey reduced drop-off at both sign-up and post-onboarding stages
✓CRM rebuilt to capture and act on customer behaviour at every lifecycle stage
✓Client retained SEvans Advisory for an additional three months post delivery
✓Cross-functional alignment established between technology and operations teams
Case Study — SEvans Advisory Engagement 02

Recruitment Capability and Process Redesign

Commercial Security ServicesTalent AcquisitionProcess Redesign
The Situation

There was no vetting framework, no defined sourcing methodology, and no consistent onboarding process. Candidate quality was inconsistent, time-to-hire was slow, and early attrition was high. An imminent UN bid added urgency to the need for qualified candidates within a tight timeframe.

The Task

Audit the existing recruitment and onboarding process. Redesign the end-to-end workflow and introduce a structured vetting framework. Source and place the Bid Manager. Identify seven qualified candidates for the UN bid. Document the process for independent operation post engagement.

Our Approach
  • Full audit of the existing recruitment and onboarding process embedded with HR and operations
  • End-to-end recruitment workflow redesigned from job brief through to candidate vetting, selection, and onboarding
  • Structured vetting framework introduced appropriate to the demands of the security sector
  • Bid Manager role sourced, assessed, and shortlisted through to successful placement
  • Seven qualified candidates identified and delivered for the UN bid within the required timeframe
  • Full process documentation produced for independent operation post engagement
The Outcome
✓Structured, repeatable recruitment process implemented replacing ad hoc hiring
✓Improved candidate quality and reduced early attrition through the new vetting framework
✓UN bid staffing requirement met in full within the required timeframe
✓Bid Manager successfully placed and onboarded
✓Client team equipped to operate the new process independently post engagement
Prior Consultant Experience

The following case studies were delivered by Silvia Evans prior to founding SEvans Advisory. They are included to demonstrate the capability and experience our founder brings to every engagement.

Case Study — Prior Consultant Experience 03

Target Operating Model Design

Wholesale BankingOperating Model DesignProgramme Delivery
The Situation

A global tier-one wholesale bank initiated a strategic programme within its Financial Markets Client Control function to address client attrition caused by a fragmented and lengthy onboarding process. The programme spanned Client Onboarding, Customer Due Diligence, Credit, Compliance, Financial Crime, and Legal but the lack of a unified operating model meant clients experienced a fragmented, slow, and often repetitive onboarding process.

The Task

Design a Target Operating Model for the FM Client Control Programme that streamlined the end-to-end client onboarding journey. The TOM needed to eliminate duplication across all functions, ensure the right information was captured once and shared effectively, and reduce the onboarding cycle time sufficiently to prevent client drop-off.

Our Approach
  • Embedded with the FM Client Control Programme to map the end-to-end client onboarding journey
  • Deep-dive analysis across international locations to establish a clear financial and operational baseline
  • Analysed each function to understand what information they required, when they needed it, and how it was currently being obtained
  • Designed a Target Operating Model that sequenced the functions correctly and ensured information was captured once and passed through the lifecycle
  • Integrated benefits realisation tracking from day one
  • Established clear intake standards eliminating repeated client contact
  • Defined protocols between functions with agreed service levels
The Outcome
✓€425K in annual operational cost savings achieved and verified
✓Leaner, more agile organisation by removing functional duplication across global locations
✓Faster account opening processes tested and refined during rollout
✓Client drop-off reduced as the more efficient onboarding process became faster and less burdensome
✓Target Operating Model received formal senior stakeholder sign-off across all functions
Case Study — Prior Consultant Experience 04

Strategic System Harmonisation Programme

Global Investment BankingBusiness AnalysisRequirements Delivery
The Situation

A major global investment bank operated multiple legacy systems across client onboarding and entity reference data that were not integrated, creating data inconsistency, duplicated effort, and material compliance risk. The programme to consolidate these into a single global strategic workflow tool was one of the largest at the bank, with very high visibility at the most senior levels of the organisation.

The Task

Lead the KYC and onboarding requirements workstream. Run workshops with geographically distributed SMEs. Produce requirements documentation and functional specifications to agreed deadlines. Ensure full traceability through design, build, and test.

Our Approach
  • Requirements workshops run with geographically distributed business Subject Matter Experts
  • Business requirements documentation and functional specifications produced to agreed deadlines
  • Full traceability of requirements maintained through design, build, and test phases
  • Status quo constructively challenged to promote operational efficiency and global standardisation
  • IT collaboration to translate business requirements into functional specifications
  • Risks, issues, and stakeholder expectations managed and escalated at programme level
  • Close collaboration with test, operational readiness, and data migration teams throughout
The Outcome
✓Comprehensive KYC and onboarding requirements delivered with full traceability
✓Requirements signed off to agreed deadlines with no material rework
✓Consolidated global workflow tool delivered replacing multiple legacy systems
✓Single source of truth established for client onboarding and entity reference data globally
✓Operational risk reduced and compliance consistency improved across all regions
Case Study — Prior Consultant Experience 05

KYC Remediation Programme

Wholesale BankingRegulatory TransformationKYC Remediation
The Situation

A global tier-one wholesale bank's KYC and client onboarding function lacked the processes, procedures, and operational infrastructure required to meet compliance standards. There was no consistent framework for conducting KYC across jurisdictions, creating regulatory risk and operational fragmentation across London, Birmingham, Bangalore, and Hong Kong.

The Task

Lead the KYC remediation programme. Design and implement end-to-end KYC processes and procedures to meet regulatory requirements. Roll out a new client onboarding system. Train teams across four international locations. Establish a globally consistent and compliant operating model.

Our Approach
  • KYC remediation programme scoped and led, establishing the full operational infrastructure required for compliance
  • End-to-end KYC processes and procedures designed in collaboration with legal, compliance, and technology teams
  • New client onboarding system implemented with user acceptance testing conducted prior to rollout
  • Teams recruited, trained, and operational across London, Birmingham, Bangalore, and Hong Kong
  • Hands-on involvement in setup of each offshore location ensuring consistent process adoption
  • Relationships managed with trading desks and sales support areas throughout transition
The Outcome
✓KYC remediation programme delivered achieving full regulatory compliance across all locations
✓End-to-end KYC processes and procedures designed, documented, and implemented
✓New client onboarding system rolled out globally with full team training completed
✓Global operational readiness achieved with all jurisdictions operating to a consistent standard
✓Operating model established became the foundation for ongoing KYC operations across all jurisdictions
Use Case

The following use case illustrates how SEvans Advisory approaches AI and automation readiness engagements.

Use Case

AI and Automation in KYC and Compliance Operations

Financial ServicesAI & Automation ReadinessKYC
The Challenge

KYC and compliance functions in financial services are among the most process-heavy, manual, and resource-intensive operations in any organisation. Repetitive data gathering, document review, and status reporting consume significant analyst time that could be redirected to higher-value judgement work.

Compounding this, the threat landscape is evolving at pace. AI-enabled fraud, deepfake identity verification bypass, synthetic identity fraud, and increasingly sophisticated social engineering attacks are emerging faster than most compliance frameworks can respond. The tools being used to commit financial crime are the same tools available to detect and prevent it. The question is which side moves faster.

The Opportunity

AI and automation tools now exist that can materially reduce operational burden while simultaneously strengthening the compliance posture. The challenge is not the technology. It is knowing where to apply it, how to sequence implementation, and how to ensure it holds up under regulatory scrutiny in an environment where both the threat and the solution set change daily.

How SEvans Advisory Approaches It

We embed within your compliance or operations function and conduct a structured process assessment, mapping every workflow, identifying repetitive tasks, and surfacing the highest-value automation candidates. We evaluate not just where AI can improve efficiency, but where it must be deployed defensively to keep pace with an evolving threat environment.

We build a prioritised roadmap ranked by value, feasibility, and regulatory risk, with one eye firmly on what is coming, not just what exists today. We are independent of any technology vendor. Our recommendations are based on your operational reality and the direction the threat landscape is moving, not on what any platform wants to sell you.

Potential Outcomes
✓Significant reduction in manual processing time across KYC workflows
✓Faster onboarding cycles without compromising compliance standards
✓Stronger fraud detection capability keeping pace with AI-enabled threats
✓Cleaner audit trails through automated documentation
✓Analyst capacity redirected to complex, judgement-intensive cases
✓A prioritised, costed roadmap ready for board-level sign-off, built for today and adaptable for tomorrow
